name: rumps (macOS Menu Bar Apps in Python)

performance.notes: Native macOS AppKit via PyObjC; responds instantly to user interaction

agent_readiness.notes: Build macOS status bar apps in Python. import rumps. class AwesomeStatusBarApp(rumps.App): def __init__(self): super().__init__('Awesome App'). @rumps.clicked('Preferences'). def prefs(self, _): rumps.alert('Preferences window'). @rumps.timer(1). def update(self, _): self.title = str(time.time()). rumps.notification('title', 'subtitle', 'message'). Menu items, separators, submenus, dynamic menus. rumps.quit_application(). App.icon for status bar icon. Requires macOS only. PyInstaller bundling for distribution. Depends on PyObjC. Mature and stable API.

gotchas: App must run in main thread; use threads for background work
PyInstaller bundling needed for distribution outside dev environment
No direct support for complex custom views in menu items
best_when: Building macOS menu bar utilities and productivity tools in Python
System monitors, timers, clipboard managers, quick launchers
Developer tools with always-accessible macOS status bar controls
When you want native macOS look without learning Swift/ObjC
avoid_when: Complex UI requirements beyond menu + notifications
Apps needing Mac App Store distribution (sandboxing issues)
summary: rumps builds macOS menu bar apps in Python. class MyApp(rumps.App): @rumps.clicked('Quit'): ... @rumps.timer(5): ... rumps.notification('title', 'sub', 'msg'). Native AppKit via PyObjC. BSD-3. macOS only. Best for status bar utilities, monitors, quick launchers. PyInstaller for distribution. No complex UI. Mature, stable API.
